titleStmt.title: Letter from Herbert W. Hawkins to the Home Secretary for Ireland, Dublin, 5 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Herbert W. Hawkins

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Herbert W. Hawkins (living in London), a keen philatelist (stamp collector), writing to the Chief Secretary's Office to obtain 'Rebel postage stamps' before they are destroyed. These stamps (or more properly labels) and their use were not sanctioned by the Post Office.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
4 Vigo Street, London W. 5.5.16.' Dear Sir, It is as a philatelist that I
beg humbly to approach you. Before all the Rebel postage
stamps are destroyed by the British
Authorities may I ask that you
will be so very considerate and
send just a set (in blocks of 4 greatly
preferred) if this can be spared and
dispatched to me entirely of your
own convenience Very best thanks in anticipation
Believe me, dear sir, Yours faithfully Herbert W Hawkins The Rt: Hon: The Home Secretary for Ireland, Dublin Inform that the Irish
Govt. have no such stamps.
